message: Morning team! Feeling pumped for the big ?Hipster Haul? event this week?
Absolutely! Can't wait to see all our hard work pay off.
Agreed, it's going to be a game-changer for us
Customer support team standing by to handle any queries. Let's do this!
Team, the excitement is palpable! I have full confidence in each of you to make this event a roaring success.
Let's showcase our best and leave our customers thrilled with what we have to offer. Together, we'll make it happen!
Absolutely, Bez! And let's not forget the potential revenue boost this event could bring. Our projections are looking promising.
:moneybag: :moneybag: :moneybag:
Fingers crossed everything goes smoothly this time! :crossed_fingers: :joy:

Welcome to Details Matter, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Speaking of celebrations, any recommendations for a post-event holiday destination? I could use some time off after this!
How about a tropical paradise with no Wi-Fi? :desert_island:
Hey folks, hate to rain on the parade, but I've got some new reports coming in about customers encountering issues with the products on the website.
Yeah, Bob, anything specific you're noticing? Error messages, affected payment methods?
Customers are not able to place orders
Alright team, let's get to work. Tinus, you can start planning that holiday once we've got this sorted!.
Deal! But first, let's get to the bottom of this issue
Hey team, just dropping in to emphasise the urgency of resolving this issue swiftly. We've got a major event on the horizon - the launch of our exclusive "Hipster Haul Weekend" next week.
It's poised to be our biggest sale yet, and any delays caused by this payment glitch could seriously impact our preparations.
Just tried a quick order seems okay so far
Let's prioritise frequent updates and keep the communication channels wide open.
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
We have a lot of recent changes made to the platform so im going to compile a list with the changes and the descriptions.
Will send it over soon
trying to place order now as well
*Incident Number INC100038:* SEV-2 Customers unable to place orders

Here's the list of all the changes that went live recently 
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YjZjNWFhYjFlZmQ0NDBmOWFlZDNlY2VjNDUxNzc4NjA
Hi team we're hearing reports of customer issues being unable to order
Investigating now
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
I will take a look and see if I can find anything in a mean time
@bob how many reports are we seeing, from all over the world or just certain regions? Certain payment methods maybe? What ties these reports all together
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
from what i can tell most of the reports seems to be coming from the two regions
My team has gathered the customer reports as of the last 3 hours to make it easier to understand
also @bob when did we start seeing issues exactly
What is going on with the website now? We are about to launch Hipster Haul
Perhaps its similar to our incident in Frankfurt with the network being down for that region?
Is this a payment outage? I need clarity fast
Okay so a somewhat regional issue it would seem. Are we seeing higher than normal traffic from those regions?
The first report was submitted 2 hours ago
It appears to maybe be localised to Japan and Turkey at this time
Investigating further!
Could be catalog or could be payment?
I will take a glance at the traffic
Well change ID CHG89873 sounds like it could be relevant if it involves an issue surrounding currency exchange from those regions
There's no surge in traffic for those 2 specific regions
Infra looks stable. No node or pod failures. Might be service-level
:star: Check changes done to the platform
We can roll it back, it looks safe
From first glance, checkout is erroring when currency conversion kicks in
@daniel can we try that please? It lines up with when issues started being reported
Change CHG89873 has been sucessfully rolledback
Hopefully that solved the issue
I made the most recent change to the currency service which is basically a security patch, I don't see how it would be causing this issue.
ill check the platform just incase it hasn't made anything worse
Card flow seems fine in GBP , but something’s off when switching currencies
Can we confirm if everythings back working?
@bob can you confirm with customers if things are working again now?
So this affects international customers only? How much revenue is that?
Not much, those two regions only make up a small percentage of revenue
We have rolled back a change in an attempt to fix so far
Reports are still pouring in, and it doesn't look good
but continuing to investigate
Hmm.... looks like that wasn't the cause
Investigating
Okay. @daniel what errors are we seeing exactly now since rolling back? What's in the logs
:star: Facts over opinion
:star: Reviewed changes
Checking logs as we speak
Wait, I switched my currency to JPY and ₺ and now the homepage errors
Looks currency-specific!
The rollback does not seemed to have fixed the issue yet
That narrows it to currencyservice or its upstream exchange provider

Good spot @shay yes it's erroring on japan and turkey currencies with this error

Uh, oh!
Something has failed. Below are some details for debugging:

HTTP Status: 500 Internal Server Error

Error Message: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing: dial tcp 172.20.164.247:7000: connect: connection refused" failed to fetch necessary data for processing

Function: main.(*dataProcessor).fetchData

File: /src/data_processor.go:124

We apologize for the inconvenience. Our system encountered an unexpected issue while attempting to retrieve essential data. Our team is actively investigating the problem. Please try your request again later.

If you need immediate assistance, please contact support.

I’m seeing this in currencyservice:
ERROR 401 Unauthorized - Exchange Rate Provider
Checkout calls currencyservice before price render
CHG89692 does contain an API key change for the provider - could this be the cause?
Maybe the new API key isn't authorised for those currencies or something
:star: Form a working theory
401 means the API key could be invalid or expired
But the outage started 2 hours after the last deploy…
Cache TTL is 12 hours. If the key was wrong but cache warm, failure would be delayed
Do we convert all currencies outside of EUR? Like GBP and CAD? Those ones seem to be working fine
It could be cache related maybe for a delayed impact
It could be an edge case, or the service is using different keys for each currency
Are we sure this isn’t Maya’s security patch from this morning?
What about CHG89254 in the currency service, could that have caused this problem?
Checking the change
We have tried rolling that back already, no effect
it seems to be breaking the site when selecting JPY or TRY currencies at the moment, others are okay
We're looking into changes around the currency service recently, there could be some caching going on which caused a delayed impact
That change is also safe to rollback, but by the looks of it, it touches all logic of all conversion so it doesn't seem like the cause
Well, let's try it and see what happens. Please roll it back @daniel
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
If the change CHG89254 was the cause, it would have affected all currencies
Understood!
CHG89254 change rolled back
Could it also be worth undoing the CHG89692 API key change if this doesn't work?
Checking the platform again
@bob how we looking now?
Do we have our best people working on it
Still nothing
Yes we got the whole team focused on this
Reports are still coming in
We've tried to roll back another change around the currency service now to see if that helps
:bulb: Who in the organisation can you escalate the issue to

Still seeing this

HTTP Status: 500 Internal Server Error

Error Message: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing: dial tcp 172.20.164.247:7000: connect: connection refused" failed to fetch necessary data for processing

Function: main.(*dataProcessor).fetchData

File: /src/data_processor.go:124

We apologize for the inconvenience. Our system encountered an unexpected issue while attempting to retrieve essential data. Our team is actively investigating the problem. Please try your request again later.

If you need immediate assistance, please contact support.
:star: Reproduces the incident
@hamed can you take a look around and see what you can find, particularly around the API key we are using for currency conversions
:star: Escalating when required
My change only updated API keys. It passed regression
I don’t see how that would suddenly fail hours later
We’re getting 401s from the FX provider now, right after cache expired
Yeah it sounds like it was cached maybe which explains why it didn't fail testing
Can we try and change this key back or get a new one that will work?
Okay, that aligns exactly with license expiry behavior
Alright… checking the secrets now
The new API key was mis-scoped. It doesn’t include JPY and TRY entitlements
So when cache cleared, the service fell back to the provider and got 401s
We think it is related to the API key for our currency conversion being updated incorrectly, but the issue was missed during testing due to caching
We are looking to change the key now
So Change 2 is the root cause
What’s the ETA to fix and will this delay Hipster Haul?
Okay, can we either roll it back or get a new key that includes those
We've identified the root cause now, it is indeed the new API key which doesn't have permission to convert for JPY and TRY
Option two sounds reasonable
we're gonna need a plan in order to do this Fix for the API keys
We are changing it now to fix this problem and should be back up in a sec
@shay go on
We need to contact the third-party currency exchange rate service provider to request a new API key.
Also will need to secure the secrets manager to store the new API key, just to make sure its not hard-coded into source code and prevent unauthorised access.
someone needs to update the configuration of the currency service to use the new API key for accessing the third-party service
What do you think @PLAYER?
How transparent are we being with customers right now?
Can we not use the old API key for now and roll back CHG89692 to get things working again or has that key already expired/been deleted?
Sadly the old keys have expired
@bob what are we communicating to customers at this time around this issue?
and okay. @hamed can you work with @daniel to get this new secret in safely and securely and quickly please, every second we're dropping customers
:star: Take command
:star: Form an effective investigation team
I'm working on the banner for the site. Is there a particular message you want me to put up or ETA?
I’ll contact the FX provider for a new licensed API key
:thumbsup:
I think just mention that JPY and TRY currencies are currently experiencing issues and they'll be fixed soon @bob
I’ll rotate the secret in Vault and lock down access
Bob has put something up on our status page to communicate to customers
No more hard-coded fallback
Sounds good
Got the new keys. Sending to you Daniel, now
Okay nice, let's get that in and fix this
Secrets updated. Permissions validated for JPY and TRY
Fix is now Implemented
Do we need to clear any caches or should we be good to go?
API keys have now been sucessfully updated
Good job, can we confirm @PLAYER?
Yep all looks working to me.
@bob
can you confirm?
It should pick up straight away with a new request
The issue appears to be resolved now, double checking with CS
Yep I just put an order through in jPY all looks good
TRY is also working
:star: Validate the fix
All currencies are working
I can see the orders going through again the regions above
Currency service reloaded. No more 401s. Rates flowing live
Fantastic, looks like we're all good t hen
Thanks everyone
Yes, issue is now resolved.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Great work. FYI team, I'll be looking into the licences for our other API keys later to make sure everything's shipshape.
What a ride! Massive thanks to each of you for your quick thinking and teamwork.
Absolutely, Shay! Getting more confident with any incidents that come our way. Good Job everyone!!
Phew, what a rollercoaster! Glad we managed to fix it before things got out of hand.
Good job everyone, but im confused how did those currencies fail recently if the change was made hours ago?
Good question @Tinus. The currency_service was using 'cache data' instead of getting the data from 3rd party service as i messed up the configuration of the APIs.
The cache data expires every 12 hours as well which must have been around the time Bob was getting the new reports.
Understood, thanks for clearing that up for me @Hamed.
Anyways with the fix in place, we've ensured that the system operates smoothly, and we're now ready once again for the big event next week.
Fantastic work, everyone! Your quick and effective response has saved the day. Now, let's channel this momentum into making our Hipster Haul Weekend a smashing success.
Thank you all for your dedication and hard work.
